canonical_question: What are the key regulatory signal sources: EPA, FDA, OSHA, SEC, state filings?
constraints: Primarily US-focused regulatory sources -- EU/UK equivalents (EMA, HSE, Companies House) require separate signal taxonomies and data pipelines
Regulatory databases are government-mandated and publicly accessible by law, but data formats, update frequencies, and API availability vary widely across agencies
Signal latency varies from real-time (SEC EDGAR) to 6-12 month delays (EPA enforcement) -- build pipelines with expected lag per source
Regulatory signals indicate compliance pressure, not purchase intent -- a company with EPA violations may remediate internally rather than hire external vendors
Cross-referencing signals across agencies multiplies value but requires entity resolution (matching company names across inconsistent government databases)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs behavioral/digital signal sources (DNS, job posts, tech stack changes)
User needs visual/physical signal sources (satellite, street-level imagery)
User needs the overall exhaust fume detection framework, not specific sources
